title: Track Uses of 'TODO' Tags in Code
description: This rule checks for any unattended 'TODO' tags in the code. 'TODO' tags are often used by developers to mark places where additional work or improvements are needed but are not implemented immediately. These tags can sometimes be overlooked or forgotten, leading to incomplete or unfinished code. The aim of this rule is to ensure that all 'TODO' tags are attended to, maintaining a clean and efficient codebase.
recommendation: Ensure to address all 'TODO' tags in your code promptly, to avoid any unfinished or incomplete code. Regularly review your codebase for any unattended 'TODO' tags and implement the necessary code or improvements marked by these tags.
impact: Unattended 'TODO' tags can lead to incomplete functionalities, missed bug fixes, hindered collaboration, and codebase bloat. Addressing these tags ensures a maintainable, readable, reliable codebase and promotes effective collaboration among developers.

checks.message: Ensure to address all 'TODO' tags in your code promptly, to avoid any unfinished or incomplete code.
